items.title: Overfitting in high-dimensional tabular models
items.description: When feature count approaches or exceeds sample size, L1/L2 penalties stabilize linear or logistic models and prevent coefficient blow-up that memorizes training noise.

items.title: Sparse interpretable models
items.description: L1 regularization produces sparse weight vectors—useful in genomics, text, and compliance settings where a compact active feature set must be reported.

items.title: Neural network generalization
items.description: L2 weight decay is standard in CNNs and transformers on limited data to keep weights small and reduce sensitivity to individual training examples.

items.title: Production hyperparameter tuning
items.description: Cross-validated λ search for L1/L2 is a routine step in ML pipelines before deploying regression, classification, or embedding models.
